question: Which trophy is given to the NHL's most valuable player each season?
explanation: The Hart Memorial Trophy has been given to the NHL MVP since 1924!

question: Which trophy is awarded to the best goaltender in the NHL each season?
explanation: The Vezina Trophy is named after Georges Vezina, a legendary Montreal Canadiens goalie from the 1920s!

question: Which award goes to the most valuable player of the Stanley Cup Playoffs?
explanation: The Conn Smythe Trophy is named after the longtime owner and coach of the Toronto Maple Leafs!

question: How many teams were part of the NHL's 'Original Six' era?
explanation: From 1942 to 1967, the NHL had just six teams: Montreal, Toronto, Boston, Chicago, Detroit, and New York Rangers!

question: Which Original Six team plays their home games at Madison Square Garden?
explanation: The Rangers have called Madison Square Garden home since 1968 — it's one of the most famous arenas in the world!

question: Which NHL team has won the most Stanley Cup championships of all time?
explanation: The Montreal Canadiens have won an incredible 24 Stanley Cups, more than any other team!

question: Which team won the Stanley Cup in their very first season in 2017-18?
explanation: The Vegas Golden Knights made it all the way to the Stanley Cup Final in their first season, though they lost. They won the Cup in 2023!

question: What is the nickname of the Detroit Red Wings and Toronto Maple Leafs rivalry?
options: The Battle of the Great Lakes
explanation: Detroit and Toronto are two Original Six teams that have battled each other since the 1920s!

question: Which player holds the NHL record for most goals in a single season with 92?
explanation: Wayne Gretzky scored 92 goals in the 1981-82 season — a record that still stands today!

question: Which award is given to the NHL's best defenseman each year?
explanation: The James Norris Memorial Trophy has honored the league's top defenseman since 1954!

question: What is the name of the trophy given to the NHL's top rookie each season?
explanation: The Calder Memorial Trophy has been awarded to the best first-year player since 1937!

question: Which team won the 2023 Stanley Cup in Las Vegas?
explanation: The Golden Knights beat the Florida Panthers to win their first Stanley Cup just six years after joining the league!

question: Which country has produced the most NHL players in history?
explanation: Canada is the birthplace of hockey, and more than half of all NHL players have been Canadian!

question: What happens during 'overtime' in an NHL regular-season game?
options: The game ends in a tie
explanation: Since 2015, NHL regular-season overtime is played 3-on-3, which creates tons of exciting scoring chances!

question: Which Washington Capitals legend is the all-time leader in NHL goals among active players?
explanation: Alex Ovechkin is chasing Wayne Gretzky's all-time goal record and has scored over 800 NHL goals!

question: What is a 'hat trick' in hockey?
explanation: When a player scores a hat trick, fans throw their hats onto the ice to celebrate!

question: Which NHL team's home arena is known as 'The Madhouse on Madison'?
explanation: The old Chicago Stadium earned that wild nickname because the fans were so loud and passionate!

question: Which team was the newest NHL franchise when they joined in 2021-22?
explanation: The Seattle Kraken became the NHL's 32nd team, named after a legendary sea creature!

question: Which Pittsburgh Penguins star wore number 87 and won three Stanley Cups?
explanation: Sidney Crosby chose #87 because he was born on 8/7/87 — August 7, 1987!

question: What is a 'power play' in hockey?
options: When one team has more players on the ice due to a penalty
When a team pulls their goalie
When a player scores from center ice
When all five skaters score in one game
correct_answer: When one team has more players on the ice due to a penalty
explanation: A power play gives one team an advantage, usually 5 players against 4, after the other team gets a penalty!

question: Which trophy goes to the NHL's leading scorer each season?
explanation: The Art Ross Trophy goes to the player with the most total points (goals plus assists) in the regular season!

question: Which team plays home games at TD Garden in Boston?
explanation: The Bruins are one of the Original Six teams and have been playing in Boston since 1924!

question: What is it called when a goalie doesn't allow any goals in a game?
explanation: Goalies love getting shutouts! Some of the best goalies in history have recorded over 100 shutouts in their careers!

question: Which Edmonton Oilers captain led his team to the 2024 Stanley Cup Final?
explanation: Connor McDavid is considered one of the fastest and most talented players in NHL history!

question: Which NHL team is famous for their 'Octopus Tradition' during the playoffs?
explanation: Red Wings fans started throwing octopuses on the ice in 1952, with eight tentacles representing the eight wins needed for the Cup back then!

question: How many periods are in a standard NHL hockey game?
explanation: Each period is 20 minutes long, so a full game has 60 minutes of playing time!

question: Which award goes to the NHL player who shows the best sportsmanship?
explanation: The Lady Byng Trophy honors the player who combines skill with gentlemanly conduct — proving you can be great AND fair!

question: Which team won back-to-back Stanley Cups in 2016 and 2017?
explanation: The Penguins, led by Sidney Crosby, won consecutive Cups, which is very hard to do in the modern NHL!

question: What do NHL teams do during a 'shootout'?
options: Players take turns shooting one-on-one against the goalie
correct_answer: Players take turns shooting one-on-one against the goalie
explanation: If overtime doesn't decide the winner, a shootout gives three players from each team a chance to score one-on-one!

question: Which team won three Stanley Cups in six years from 2010 to 2015?
explanation: The Blackhawks built a dynasty by winning the Cup in 2010, 2013, and 2015!

question: What is the area in front of the goal where goalies play most often called?
explanation: The crease is painted blue and is the goaltender's special area — skaters have to be careful not to interfere there!

question: Which NHL trophy is awarded to the best forward who plays strong defense?
explanation: The Frank J. Selke Trophy goes to the forward who is best at the defensive side of the game!

question: Which legendary goalie holds the NHL record with 691 career wins?
explanation: Martin Brodeur played 22 NHL seasons, mostly with the New Jersey Devils, and is the winningest goalie ever!

question: What is the line at center ice called?
explanation: The red line divides the ice into two halves and is used for icing calls!

question: Which Florida team won their first-ever Stanley Cup in 2024?
explanation: The Panthers came back from a 3-0 series deficit the year before and finally got their first Cup in 2024!

question: What is the penalty box sometimes nicknamed?
explanation: Players who get penalties sit in the 'sin bin' for usually 2 or 5 minutes depending on the penalty!

question: Which team won back-to-back Stanley Cups in 2020 and 2021?
explanation: The Lightning dominated those two seasons, winning the Cup in the bubble in 2020 and again in 2021!

question: Which player was the first overall pick in the 2015 NHL Draft and became a superstar in Edmonton?
explanation: Connor McDavid was considered a generational talent and went first overall to the Edmonton Oilers!

question: What does it mean when a goalie is 'pulled' from the game?
options: The goalie leaves for an extra skater
correct_answer: The goalie leaves for an extra skater
explanation: Teams pull their goalie when they're losing late in the game to get an extra attacker — it's a risky but exciting move!

question: Which trophy is awarded to the goal-scoring leader in the NHL each season?
explanation: Named after the legendary 'Rocket' Richard, the first player to score 50 goals in a single season!

question: Which team won the 2023 Stanley Cup in just their 6th season as a franchise?
explanation: The Vegas Golden Knights won the Cup in 2023, beating the Florida Panthers!

question: What is a 'Gordie Howe hat trick'?
options: A goal, an assist, and a fight in one game
correct_answer: A goal, an assist, and a fight in one game
explanation: It's named after the tough and talented Gordie Howe, who could score AND stand up for his teammates!

question: Which Original Six team is known for having the longest active Stanley Cup drought?
explanation: The Maple Leafs last won the Stanley Cup in 1967 — that's a long wait for their passionate fans!

question: What is 'icing' in hockey?
options: Shooting the puck from your side past the opponent's goal line
When the ice gets too slippery
correct_answer: Shooting the puck from your side past the opponent's goal line
explanation: Icing stops play and brings the faceoff back to the defending team's zone — it keeps teams from just dumping the puck!

question: Which player was drafted first overall in 2016 and scored in his NHL debut for Toronto?
explanation: Auston Matthews scored FOUR goals in his very first NHL game — the only player in the modern era to do that!

question: How many players from each team are usually on the ice during regular play?
explanation: Each team has 5 skaters (3 forwards and 2 defensemen) plus a goaltender on the ice!

question: Which team's mascot is a large green creature named Gritty?
explanation: Gritty became an instant internet sensation when he debuted in 2018 with his crazy eyes and wild personality!

question: What is it called when a player scores a goal while their team is short-handed due to a penalty?
explanation: Shorthanded goals are rare and exciting because the scoring team actually has fewer players on the ice!

question: Which famous rivalry is between the Montreal Canadiens and the Boston Bruins?
options: The oldest rivalry in the NHL
correct_answer: The oldest rivalry in the NHL
explanation: Montreal and Boston have been rivals since the 1920s and have met in the playoffs more than 30 times!

question: Which player holds the record for most career penalty minutes in NHL history?
explanation: Tiger Williams racked up 3,966 penalty minutes over his career — that's like spending almost 3 full days in the penalty box!

question: What trophy is awarded to the team with the best regular-season record in MLS?
explanation: The Supporters' Shield has been given out since 1999 and is voted on as the most consistent team award!

question: What is the annual award given to the MLS top scorer called?
explanation: The Golden Boot goes to whichever player scores the most goals during the MLS regular season!

question: What is the rivalry match between LAFC and the LA Galaxy called?

question: What is the name of the rivalry between the New York Red Bulls and New York City FC?
explanation: Named after the famous Hudson River, this rivalry between NYC's two MLS teams is always intense!

question: Which famous English player joined the LA Galaxy in 2007 and helped grow soccer in America?
explanation: David Beckham's arrival was a huge deal and is often called the moment that changed MLS forever!

question: What is a 'Designated Player' in MLS?
options: A star player whose salary doesn't fully count against the salary cap
A player who plays every position
correct_answer: A star player whose salary doesn't fully count against the salary cap
explanation: The Designated Player rule, also called the 'Beckham Rule,' lets teams sign big-name stars!

question: Which team won the very first MLS Cup in 1996?
explanation: D.C. United won the first MLS Cup and went on to win it again in 1997 and 1999!

question: Which MLS team has a famous supporters' group called the 'Timbers Army'?
explanation: The Timbers Army is known for their incredible chants, flags, and a real lumberjack who cuts a log slab after every goal!

question: Which MLS team has won the most MLS Cup titles?
explanation: The LA Galaxy have won five MLS Cup titles, making them the most successful team in league history!

question: What is the oldest ongoing soccer tournament in the United States?
explanation: The Lamar Hunt US Open Cup started in 1914 and is open to teams from all levels of American soccer!

question: Which team joined MLS in 2023 and plays in St. Louis?
explanation: St. Louis City SC had an amazing first season, finishing with the best record in the Western Conference!

question: Which Argentine superstar joined Inter Miami in 2023?
explanation: Messi chose Inter Miami after winning the World Cup with Argentina, bringing massive excitement to MLS!

question: Which MLS team is known for their famous 'March to the Match' fan tradition?
explanation: Sporting KC fans march together from a local gathering spot all the way to the stadium before every home game!

question: Which Seattle team has won two MLS Cup titles and is known for their loud fans?

question: What is the CONCACAF Champions Cup?
options: A tournament between the best clubs from North America, Central America, and the Caribbean
A tournament for national teams only
correct_answer: A tournament between the best clubs from North America, Central America, and the Caribbean
explanation: MLS teams compete against clubs from Mexico, Central America, and the Caribbean to be the best in the region!

question: Which team broke the MLS single-season points record in 2019 with 73 points?

question: Which team won the MLS Cup in their second-ever season in 2018?
explanation: Atlanta United won it all in just their second year, with over 73,000 fans at the final in Mercedes-Benz Stadium!

question: How many teams are currently in Major League Soccer?
explanation: MLS has grown from 10 teams in 1996 to 29 teams, with San Diego joining in 2025!

question: Which rivalry between the Seattle Sounders and Portland Timbers is called what?
explanation: The Cascadia Cup also includes the Vancouver Whitecaps and is named after the Cascadia region of the Pacific Northwest!

question: Which Colombian striker became the first MLS player to win the league MVP and Golden Boot in the same season for Atlanta United?

question: Which team was the first MLS club to win the CONCACAF Champions League?
explanation: The Seattle Sounders made history in 2022 by becoming the first MLS team to win the continental championship!

question: What is it called when a player scores three goals in one soccer match?
explanation: Hat tricks are rare and exciting in soccer — some players go entire seasons without scoring three in one game!

question: Which MLS team plays at Yankee Stadium in New York?
explanation: NYCFC played at the famous baseball stadium while waiting for their own soccer-specific stadium to be built!

question: What is a 'clean sheet' in soccer?
options: When a goalkeeper doesn't allow any goals
When a team wins by 3 or more goals
When a player gets no yellow cards
When a team has no fouls
correct_answer: When a goalkeeper doesn't allow any goals
explanation: Goalkeepers take a lot of pride in getting clean sheets — it means their defense was perfect that game!

question: Which MLS team is co-owned by David Beckham?
explanation: David Beckham helped found Inter Miami as part of a deal he made when he first came to play in MLS!

question: Which player holds the MLS all-time career goals record?

question: What award is given to the MLS Most Valuable Player each season?
explanation: The MVP award is named after Landon Donovan, one of the greatest American soccer players ever!

question: What color card results in an immediate ejection from a soccer match?

question: Which team won the 2023 MLS Cup championship?
explanation: The Columbus Crew won their third MLS Cup title, beating LAFC in the final!

question: What is VAR in MLS matches?

question: Which team was one of the original 10 MLS teams and plays in Columbus, Ohio?

question: Which legendary Italian player won the MLS MVP with New York City FC in 2016?

question: What happens in MLS when the score is tied after regulation in a playoff game?
options: Two 15-minute extra time periods are played
The game goes straight to penalties
The game ends in a draw
They replay the match another day
correct_answer: Two 15-minute extra time periods are played
explanation: If it's still tied after extra time, the game goes to a dramatic penalty shootout!

question: Which expansion team joined MLS in 2021 and plays in Austin, Texas?
explanation: Austin FC joined MLS in 2021 and quickly built a passionate fanbase in the Texas capital!

question: Who is the all-time leading scorer for the US Men's National Team and was also an MLS star?
explanation: Landon Donovan scored 57 goals for the US national team and won 6 MLS Cup titles with the LA Galaxy!

question: What do two yellow cards in the same game equal?
explanation: If a player gets two yellow cards in the same match, they automatically receive a red card and are sent off!

question: Which MLS team plays at Mercedes-Benz Stadium and set attendance records?
explanation: Atlanta United regularly draws over 70,000 fans, making them one of the best-attended soccer clubs in the world!

question: What is the SuperDraft in MLS?
options: An event where teams pick college and young players
correct_answer: An event where teams pick college and young players

question: Which MLS team won the Leagues Cup in 2023, a tournament between MLS and Liga MX teams?

question: What position does the player who wears gloves and guards the net play?

question: Which team is named after a famous firebird and plays in Houston?

question: What is 'stoppage time' or 'injury time' in soccer?

A timeout called by the coach
correct_answer: Extra minutes added to the end of a half for delays
explanation: The referee adds time for injuries, substitutions, and other stoppages — that's why soccer games don't end exactly at 90 minutes!

question: Which team beat the New York Red Bulls to win the 2024 MLS Cup?

A tackle that wins the ball
A header that clears the ball

question: Which MLS team plays in a stadium named after a famous ketchup brand in Cincinnati?

question: What award is given to the MLS Rookie of the Year?

question: Which Canadian city's MLS team is called the Whitecaps?

question: What is the Campeones Cup?

question: Which MLS team was saved from relocating after a huge fan campaign called 'Save the Crew'?

question: Which bowl game is known as 'The Granddaddy of Them All'?

question: Which rivalry game between Ohio State and Michigan is played every year at the end of the regular season?

question: What is the name of the rivalry game between Alabama and Auburn?

question: Which annual rivalry game features the Army Black Knights vs. the Navy Midshipmen?

question: How many teams made the first expanded College Football Playoff in the 2024-25 season?

question: Which SEC school's mascot is a live tiger named Mike?

question: Which coach won six national championships at the University of Alabama?

question: What is the conference that includes schools like Ohio State, Michigan, and Penn State?

question: Which team's fans plant a flag at midfield and are known as the 'Sooner Schooner'?

question: What is a 'bowl game' in college football?

question: What do Notre Dame fans call their student section?

question: Which Georgia quarterback won the Heisman Trophy in 2024?

question: What tradition happens at the end of the Army-Navy game?

The winning team does a lap around the field
The losing team salutes the crowd
correct_answer: Both teams stand together and sing each other's alma mater

question: Which SEC team is known for their 'War Eagle' battle cry?

question: Which team won back-to-back national championships in 2022 and 2023?

question: What is a 'Hail Mary' pass in football?

A short pass to the tight end

question: Which school is known for 'Midnight Yell' the night before every home game?

question: Which team is known as the 'Crimson Tide'?

question: Which team plays at 'The Horseshoe,' officially called Ohio Stadium?

question: What is it called when the home crowd gets so loud it feels like the stadium is shaking?

question: Which Michigan Wolverines coach led the team to the 2024 national championship before leaving for the NFL?

question: What is the NCAA basketball tournament nicknamed?

question: What is it called when a low-seeded team beats a much higher-seeded team in March Madness?

question: How many teams make it to the Final Four in the NCAA Tournament?

question: What award is given to the best player in college basketball each year?

question: Which rivalry is considered the greatest in college basketball?

question: What is a 'bracket' in March Madness?

question: What happens when the shot clock runs out in college basketball?

The team gets a free throw

question: How many regions are in the NCAA Tournament bracket?

question: What is a 'buzzer beater' in basketball?

A player who gets fouled at the buzzer
A timeout called at the last second
A fast break at the end of a quarter
correct_answer: A shot that goes in right as time expires

question: Which Kentucky tradition involves fans rushing the court after a big win?

question: Which famous coach led UCLA to 10 national championships?

question: What is a 'one-and-done' player in college basketball?

A player who only plays one game

correct_answer: A player who goes to the NBA after one college season

question: Which team is known as the 'Wildcats' and plays their home games at Rupp Arena?
explanation: Rupp Arena is named after legendary coach Adolph Rupp and holds over 20,000 fans for Kentucky basketball!

question: How many total teams are in the NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament field?
explanation: The tournament expanded to 68 teams in 2011, with the First Four play-in games before the main bracket!

question: Which team did coach Dan Hurley lead to back-to-back national titles in 2023 and 2024?
explanation: Dan Hurley turned UConn into a powerhouse, with the Huskies dominating the tournament two years in a row!

question: What is 'Selection Sunday' in college basketball?
options: The day the tournament bracket is revealed
The first day of the tournament
The day teams pick their jerseys
correct_answer: The day the tournament bracket is revealed
explanation: On Selection Sunday, 68 teams find out if they made the tournament — it's a super exciting day for fans and players!

question: Which school's basketball arena is called the Phog Allen Fieldhouse?
explanation: Allen Fieldhouse is named after Phog Allen, who coached Kansas for 39 years and is called the 'Father of Basketball Coaching'!

question: What is the lowest seed to ever reach the Final Four?
explanation: Several 11 seeds have reached the Final Four, including Loyola-Chicago in 2018 with their famous 98-year-old fan, Sister Jean!

question: Which college basketball conference is known as the 'toughest in the nation' and includes Kentucky and Tennessee?
explanation: The SEC has become a basketball powerhouse with teams like Kentucky, Tennessee, Auburn, and Alabama competing at the highest level!

question: What happens when a player commits five fouls in a college basketball game?
options: They are disqualified and can't play the rest of the game
They sit out for five minutes
correct_answer: They are disqualified and can't play the rest of the game
explanation: In college basketball, five fouls and you're out — in the NBA, it takes six fouls!

question: Which small Jesuit school from Indiana became a Cinderella story with Sister Jean in 2018?
explanation: Loyola-Chicago's 98-year-old chaplain Sister Jean became a national celebrity as the Ramblers made an incredible Final Four run!

question: How long is the three-point line from the basket in men's college basketball?
explanation: The college three-point line is slightly shorter than the NBA line, which is 23 feet 9 inches!

question: Which team won the 2022 NCAA Championship with coach Hubert Davis?
explanation: Kansas came back from a 16-point deficit to beat North Carolina in one of the greatest title game comebacks ever!

question: What is the 'transfer portal' in college basketball?
options: A system where players can move to another school
A new type of basketball play
correct_answer: A system where players can move to another school
explanation: The transfer portal changed college basketball by making it much easier for players to switch schools!

question: How many halves are in a college basketball game?
explanation: College basketball plays two 20-minute halves, while the NBA plays four 12-minute quarters!

question: Which team that had never won a championship shocked everyone by winning it all in 2023?
explanation: UConn won the 2023 title by dominating every opponent, winning all six tournament games by double digits!

question: Which number one seed has famously NEVER lost to a 16 seed before UMBC did it in 2018?
explanation: Virginia became the first-ever 1 seed to lose to a 16 seed when UMBC beat them 74-54 — the biggest upset in tournament history!

question: Which school is famous for always being competitive despite being a small school in Spokane, Washington?
explanation: Gonzaga went from a little-known school to a national powerhouse, making the tournament over 20 straight years!

question: What tradition do teams do after winning the NCAA championship game?
options: Throw the basketball into the crowd
Carry the coach on a chair
Do a victory lap around the court
explanation: After winning a championship, players and coaches take turns climbing a ladder and cutting pieces of the net as souvenirs!

question: Which conference tournament is held at Madison Square Garden in New York City?
explanation: The Big East Tournament at MSG is one of the most exciting events in college basketball, with passionate fans packing the arena!

question: What did Virginia do the year AFTER being upset by 16-seed UMBC?
options: Lost in the first round again
explanation: Virginia had the ultimate comeback story — after the worst loss in tournament history, they came back and won it all in 2019!
